strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.message: Design the target phrase before AI voice leading is generated.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.preview_selectio: Selected-measure mode is unavailable here, so current measure will be used.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.role_desc.cadent: Strengthen cadential resolution with convincing harmonic closure.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.role_desc.climax: Aim for the strongest arrival with heightened tension and clear directional lift.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.role_desc.contra: Add contrast in register, rhythm, or shape while staying harmonically coherent.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.role_desc.develo: Maintain forward motion with balanced variety and phrase continuity.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.role_desc.expans: Expand the line gradually with broader span and richer variation.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.role_desc.intro: Stable register, open breathing, low tension, and clear opening color.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.role_desc.outro_: Shape the ending with a final release or lingering coda color.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.role_desc.pre_cl: Build energy toward the peak with rising direction and increasing density.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.role_desc.releas: Relax the phrase after tension, reduce leaps, and smooth the cadence path.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.role_desc.sequen: Use motivic repetition and stepwise sequential motion across the phrase.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.role_desc.theme_: Support the main theme with singable contour and clear arrival points.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.runtime_desc.aut: Use the current profile automatically. Good default unless you want to compare engines directly.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.runtime_desc.onn: ONNX Runtime inference. Better path for future optimization and easier CPU/GPU scaling.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.runtime_desc.tin: Lightweight built-in GRU inference. Usually the most stable option for small local models.
strings.dialog.voice_leading.ai.title: AI Voice Leading Phrase Designer

strings.help.title: Help
strings.help.topic.compose.body:
Compose Assist

[Enable]
- Toggle "Compose" in top bar
- Modes: Functional/Pop/Jazz/Emotional/Circle5

[Melody Variation]
- Transforms selected notes (inversion, retrograde, augmentation, etc.)
- Snapped to current key scale

[Rhythm Pattern]
- Re-positions notes to match a pattern
- Patterns: recommended, by genre, custom

[Chord Suggestions]
- Analyzes melody to infer chords per measure
- Generates voice-led chord voicings

[Compose Overlay]
- Note Guide / Chord Suggestion text follows the current UI language

[Voice Leading]
- Generates melodic lines connecting chords
- 11 tunable parameters (Settings dialog)

[Accompaniment Track]
- Styles: Arpeggio / Chord Beat / Drum Groove
- From melody (Track 0) or key-based fallback
- Also available in left palette panel

[AI Compose]
- AI prompt panel for AI-assisted composing
- Scope: Next Measure / Selected / Whole Song
- Requires external AI server (Settings)

[Melodic Mood Overlay]
- Shows stability/transition/tension on notes
- 3 color styles: Jewel/Muted/Editorial
strings.help.topic.compose.title: Compose
strings.help.topic.files.body:
File Management

[Commands]  (in General palette)
- New: creates 1 Piano track, 8 measures
- Open: loads .json / .arkscore files
- Save / Save As

[Multi-Document Tabs]
- Open multiple scores in separate tabs
- "+" button to create new tab
- Click tab to switch, X to close
- Each tab has independent undo/redo

[Export]
- Score: PDF / PNG / JPG
- Audio: WAV (lossless) / MP3 (compressed)
  MP3 requires lame.exe in Tools/ folder

[File Format]
- JSON format (.json / .arkscore)
- Stores: tracks, instruments, measures, notes, lyrics, expression marks, title

[Language Settings]
- General > "Language Settings"
- English / Korean supported
- Custom languages: add JSON to Language/

[Troubleshooting]
- No sound? Check SoundFont and track settings
- Track hidden? Use "Show Track" command
- MP3 fail? Check lame.exe in Tools/ folder
strings.help.topic.files.title: Files
strings.help.topic.leftdock.body:
Left Dock & Palette

[Layout]
- Top: Tool palette (categorized sections)
- Bottom: Player controls (fixed height)
- Collapse button (<</>>) to minimize

[Tool Categories]
- General: File, Edit, Undo/Redo, Track, Compose Assist, Language, Lyrics, Overlay
- Compose Assist: Melody/Rhythm/Chord/Voice
- Accompaniment: Style + action buttons
- Clef: Treble, Bass
- Time: 4/4, 3/4, 2/4, 6/8, 12/8
- Expression: accel./rit./p/mf/f/cresc./dim.
- Key: Major/Minor x 15 keys x 3 apply modes
- Notes: Accidentals + Note durations (dotted)
- Rests: Rest durations

[Pinned Tools]
- Frequently used tools appear in top bar

[Settings]
- Default velocity: 100 (0-127)
- Grid quantize: 1/16th note (0.25 beats)
strings.help.topic.leftdock.title: Left Dock
strings.help.topic.modes.body:
Editor Modes (top bar)

[Select Measures]
- Left click/drag to select measure ranges
- Shift+click to add measures to selection
- Selected measures are auto-copied to clipboard

[Select Notes]
- Left click/drag to select individual notes
- Shift+click to add notes to selection
- Selected notes are auto-copied to clipboard

[Edit]
- Left click applies the current tool (note/rest/etc.)
- Ghost preview follows mouse cursor
- Drag converts to selection automatically
  - Drag from note or Shift+drag: note selection
  - Otherwise: measure selection
- Alt+click on a note: delete immediately
strings.help.topic.modes.title: Modes
strings.help.topic.playback.body:
Playback

[Player Controls]  (bottom of left dock)
- Play / Pause / Stop buttons
- Position slider to seek
- Volume slider (0 ~ 100%)

[Playback Behavior]
- If measures are selected, plays only that range
- If no selection, plays the entire song
- Playback cursor (red line) tracks position
- Hidden tracks are excluded from playback
- accel./rit. marks affect tempo during playback

[SoundFont]
- Place .sf2 files in SoundFonts/ folder
- Falls back to basic synth if no SF2 found

[Audio Export]
- WAV: lossless audio file
- MP3: compressed (requires lame.exe in Tools/)
strings.help.topic.playback.title: Playback
strings.help.topic.selection.body:
Selection Details

[Measure Selection]
- Click or drag to select measures
- Shift+click adds to selection
- "Clear Selection" replaces notes with rests
  (measure structure is preserved)
- "Delete" removes entire measures
  (subsequent measures shift forward)

[Note Selection]
- Click or drag to select notes
- Shift+click adds to selection
- "Delete" removes notes (replaced by rests)

[Note Drag - Pitch Change]
- Long-press a note (~600ms), then drag up/down
- Pitch changes in real time while dragging
- Entire drag is a single undo step

[Paste Mode]
- Ctrl+V activates paste preview
- Mouse hover shows ghost of clipboard notes
- Left click pastes at that position
- Notes overflow into next measures if needed
- Right-click or Esc to exit paste mode
- Drag selection auto-copies to clipboard
strings.help.topic.selection.title: Selection
strings.help.topic.shortcuts.body:
Keyboard Shortcuts

[Edit]
Ctrl+Z          Undo
Ctrl+Y          Redo
Delete          Delete selected notes/measures
Ctrl+C          Copy selection to clipboard
Ctrl+V          Enter paste mode

[Selection]
Escape          Clear selection (or exit paste mode)
Right-click     Quick-select note or measure

[Transpose / Move]
Up Arrow        Transpose +1 semitone
Down Arrow      Transpose -1 semitone
Ctrl+Up         Diatonic step up in current key
Ctrl+Down       Diatonic step down in current key
Left/Right      Move selected notes left/right

[View]
Mouse wheel          Scroll
Ctrl + mouse wheel   Zoom (0.5x ~ 1.6x)
strings.help.topic.shortcuts.title: Shortcuts
strings.help.topic.tools.body:
Input Tools (left palette)

[Note]  Whole / Half / Quarter / 8th / 16th / 32nd
- Each duration has a dotted variant (adds 50%)
- Click on score to insert at that pitch and beat
- Notes at the same beat stack into chords
- Inserted note plays a preview sound

[Rest]  Whole / Half / Quarter / 8th / 16th / 32nd
- Click on score to insert rest at that beat

[Accidental]  Flat(b) / Natural / Sharp(#)
- Click on an existing note to apply

[Time Signature]  4/4, 3/4, 2/4, 6/8, 12/8
- Click on a measure to set its time signature

[Key Signature]  Major/Minor, fifths -7..+7
- Three apply modes: Pitch-fixed / Position-fixed / Transpose

[Clef]  Treble / Bass
- Applies to the entire track

[Expression Marks]
- Tempo: accel. (speed up), rit. (slow down)
- Dynamics: p, mf, f, cresc., dim.
- Click on score to insert at that position
- Tempo marks affect playback speed
strings.help.topic.tools.title: Tools
strings.help.topic.track.body:
Track Management

[Add / Delete Track]
- "Track Add" creates a new track
- "Track Delete" shows dialog to choose which
  track to delete (cannot delete the last track)

[Hide / Show Track]
- "Hide Track": hide selected track from view and playback
- "Show Track": make hidden track visible again
- At least one track must remain visible

[Track Instrument Settings]
  Click track header to open dialog:
- Track Name, SoundFont, Instrument
- Program (0-127), MIDI Channel
- Bank MSB/LSB, Percussion, Volume, Pan

[Tempo]
- Click tempo mark (e.g. J=120) on score
- Enter BPM (20~300), applies to all tracks

[Lyrics]
- Double-click measure to edit lyrics
- "Lyrics View/Edit" for bulk editing
  (/lyrics/per/measure/ format)
strings.help.topic.track.title: Track

strings.voice.help.common_tone: Common Tone Hold\nMeaning: Weight for preserving shared pitch classes across harmony changes.\nEffect: Higher = steadier connected voicing, Lower = more active note changes.
strings.voice.help.ending_cadence_bars: Ending Cadence Bars\nMeaning: Number of bars reserved for forced ending progression.\nEffect: Larger = longer guided cadence, Smaller = shorter close.
strings.voice.help.ending_cadence_force: Ending Cadence Force\nMeaning: Strength that forces the final section to resolve with cadential progression.\nEffect: Higher = clearer ending (dominant to tonic), Lower = freer ending color.
strings.voice.help.leap_comp: Leap Compensation\nMeaning: Strength of opposite step motion after a leap.\nEffect: Higher = quicker recovery to smooth motion, Lower = freer continuation.
strings.voice.help.max_leap: Max Leap\nMeaning: Maximum interval jump between adjacent melody notes.\nEffect: Higher = wider/dramatic jumps, Lower = smoother connected line.
strings.voice.help.mode: Mode Preset\nMeaning: Selects the base voice-leading character (Pop/Jazz/Emotional).\nEffect: Changes the default balance between stability, tension, and resolution.
strings.voice.help.opening_key_anchor: Opening Key Anchor\nMeaning: Strength that forces opening measures to start from key-centered harmony and melody.\nEffect: Higher = more stable tonic opening, Lower = freer intro chords.
strings.voice.help.resolution: Resolution Strictness\nMeaning: How strictly unstable tones resolve.\nEffect: Higher = stronger cadential pull and predictability, Lower = looser modern motion.
strings.voice.help.stepwise: Stepwise Bias\nMeaning: Preference for moving by small steps (1-2 semitones).\nEffect: Higher = singable/connected phrases, Lower = more skips.
strings.voice.help.strong_beat: Strong Beat Chord Tone\nMeaning: Bias toward chord tones on strong beats.\nEffect: Higher = clearer harmonic grounding, Lower = more freedom on accents.
strings.voice.help.tension: Tension Use\nMeaning: Amount of non-chord/tension color in generated line.\nEffect: Higher = richer/jazzier color, Lower = cleaner triadic melody.
